preamble.summary: Joseph interprets the dreams of the cupbearer and baker. Two years later, Pharaoh dreams. Joseph interprets and is elevated to rule over Egypt.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees compresses Genesis 40-41 into a brief account, focusing on providential pattern rather than narrative drama.
preamble.friction: The brevity is striking compared to Genesis's extensive court scene.

verses.text_reference: And the chief butler and baker offended the king and were put in ward.
verses.rendering: The king of Egypt's chief cupbearer and chief baker offended the king. He put them in custody in the prison where Joseph was confined.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph was appointed to serve them.
verses.rendering: The captain of the guard assigned Joseph to attend them, and he served in their presence.

verses.text_reference: And they both dreamed and told their dreams to Joseph.
verses.rendering: Both men dreamed on the same night and told their dreams to Joseph.

verses.text_reference: And as he interpreted, so it befell them.
verses.rendering: Things happened exactly as Joseph interpreted: Pharaoh restored the cupbearer and executed the baker.

verses.text_reference: But the butler forgot Joseph, until Pharaoh dreamed two dreams.
verses.rendering: But the chief cupbearer forgot Joseph in prison until Pharaoh dreamed two dreams in a single night and no one could interpret them.

verses.text_reference: And then the butler remembered Joseph.
verses.rendering: Then the cupbearer remembered Joseph. He spoke about him to the king, and Joseph was brought from prison.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph said: 'Seven years of plenty, then seven of famine.'
verses.rendering: Joseph told Pharaoh: Seven years of abundance are coming, followed by seven years of famine — the likes of which the land has never seen.

verses.text_reference: And he commanded Pharaoh to appoint overseers.
verses.rendering: He advised Pharaoh to appoint overseers throughout Egypt to store food during the years of plenty. Pharaoh was pleased.

verses.text_reference: And the king set Joseph over all the land of Egypt.
verses.rendering: The king placed Joseph over all the land of Egypt. He became ruler over all officials and over the entire land. Everything was done according to Joseph's word, for the Lord was with him.
